strengths: Thorough, timestamped client consultations demonstrating a deep understanding of the problem
Comprehensive and measurable success criteria covering all major functional areas
weaknesses: Product rationale, while sound, lacks a full evaluation of alternative technologies and trade-offs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (scenario investigation and success criteria) clearly meet the top band, but the rationale is only partially justified (3â4 band). Overall this criterion sits just below full marks at 5/6.

strengths: High level of complexity: authentication, role-based menus, dynamic content, transaction rollbacks
Appropriate and effective use of professional tools (Tomcat, MySQL, Bootstrap, JSP, JSTL)
weaknesses: Minimal inline documentation and no citation of external sources or version control history
Lacks evidence of automated build/deployment processes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strong complexity and good use of tools (bands 8â12 and 5â8) are tempered by very limited documentation (1â4). The weighted balance places the criterion in the mid-higher range at 6/12.

strengths: Implicit MVC structure inferred from code shows some design thinking
weaknesses: No record of tasks or timeline to demonstrate development progress
No explicit test plan or documented test cases
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With two strands scoring zero (task record, test plan) and only a minimal design overview (1â2 band), the holistic performance is very weak, meriting 1/6.

strengths: All required functions operate correctly as confirmed by client alpha testing
Clear, layered architecture allowing straightforward future expansion
weaknesses: No direct video evidence provided to corroborate functionality demonstration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands sit squarely in the top band. The product functions well and is designed for extensibility, justifying the full 4/4.

strengths: Realistic and well-linked future development recommendations
weaknesses: Self-evaluation against success criteria is brief and lacks quantitative depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Client feedback fully meets the 5â6 band and recommendations are solid (3â4 band), but the evaluation against success criteria remains only partial (3â4 band). Overall this merits 4/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: A partial design emerges implicitly from the code (multi-tier MVC structure, servlet mappings, JSP front-end) but there are **no explicit diagrams, data models or annotated wire-frames**.  This demonstrates only a limited overview of how the product was conceived, placing it in the 1-2 band.

The rationale explains why a web-based system (Java + MySQL + JSP) is suitable: it is free, well-supported and familiar to the developer, and will be intuitive for non-technical staff.  While this shows a **sound link between technology, problem and client**, it stops short of a thorough justification that weighs alternative technologies or constraints, so the strand aligns best with the 3-4 band rather than full marks.

The student makes **effective use of multiple professional tools**: Eclipse/IntelliJ project, Apache Tomcat, MySQL, JDBC connection pooling, Bootstrap front-end and JSTL.  Use is appropriate and purposeful, but evidence of automated build, version control or advanced libraries is missing; hence the upper mid-band (8).
The submission contains functional code but **no outline test plan**âno table of test cases, expected results or references to traceabilityâhence the strand receives zero.

Recommendations captured from the client (student feedback feature, notifications, mobile app, cancellation workflow) are **realistic and clearly linked to future development**, but they originate mainly from the client rather than a critical student analysis, so a solid 3-4 band score is appropriate.

Code is lengthy yet largely **undocumented and contains no citation of external tutorials or libraries** beyond standard dependencies.  Minimal inline comments and no bibliography indicate only limited documentation, placing it at the bottom of the 1-4 band.
No task list or Gantt-style record is included.  Consequently, there is **no evidence** of planning or iterative progress tracking, so the strand scores zero.

The solution spans user authentication, role segregation, dynamic menu generation with randomness and date logic, CRUD operations for multiple entities, payment transactions with rollback, price history management and JSP-based GUI.  Integrating servlets, JDBC, session management and validation reflects **considerable complexity and ingenuity** but a few routines remain fairly standard; a mid-top-band score of 10 is therefore appropriate.

Fourteen explicit and measurable success criteria cover authentication, role-based dashboards, CRUD operations, ticket generation, automatic calculations, data validation and UI elements.  They are **comprehensive, specific and entirely relevant** to judging the finished product, satisfying the highest band.

Extensive, itemised feedback from the client after alpha testing and a separate section for improvement suggestions provides **comprehensive qualitative evidence** of adviser input, fully satisfying the top band.

Layered architecture (controllers, services, DAOs) and clear entity classes mean new functionality (e.g., feedback, notifications) can be hooked in with modest effort.  This demonstrates **straightforward extensibility**, meriting the upper band.

The student presents a clearlyâdefined problem (canteen administration) for a real client, Ms Phuong Anh, and includes two separate, timestamped interview transcripts plus follow-up discussion when confirming success criteria.  This constitutes **detailed, first-hand evidence of consultation** that demonstrates an in-depth understanding of the clientâs needs, thereby meeting the requirements of the top band.

Clientâs alpha-testing feedback confirms that all listed functions operate correctly; video evidence is implied but not supplied.  Given the breadth of working features, the product appears to **function well overall**, justifying the top band of criterion C (4 marks).

The student records yes/no outcomes for each success criterion, showing a **structured but brief self-evaluation**; however, there is little reflection on shortcomings or quantitative testing, so the work sits in the 3-4 band rather than 5-6.
